title: Bacon & Kale Breakfast Hash
time: Prep: 10 min | Cook: 30 min | Total: 40 min
bodyHtml:
Protein: Breakfast (Bacon)Style: One PanTime: Prep: 10 min | Cook: 30 min

🛡️ Medical Protocol Adaptations (Low-FODMAP)

  • Sweet Potato: Substituted with peeled Yukon Gold potatoes per protocol.
  • Mushrooms: Substituted with shredded carrots to keep it strictly Low-FODMAP.
  • Aromatics: Used green onion tops (green parts only) and chives.

Ingredients

  • 8-10 slices Bacon (nitrate-free, chopped)
  • 2 lbs Yukon Gold Potatoes (peeled and diced into 1/4 inch cubes)
  • 1 cup Shredded Carrots
  • 2 cups Kale (chopped, stems removed)
  • 1/4 cup Green Onion tops or Chives (sliced)
  • 2 tbsp Fresh Parsley (chopped)
  • 2 tbsp Fresh Cilantro (chopped)
  • 3/4 tsp Sea Salt (plus more to taste)

Instructions

  1. Crisp Bacon: Heat a large cast iron skillet over medium heat. Add chopped bacon and cook until crispy. Remove bacon with a slotted spoon, leaving the fat in the pan.
  2. Brown Potatoes: Add diced potatoes to the skillet in an even layer. Cook in the bacon fat for 12-15 minutes, stirring infrequently to allow them to caramelize and brown.
  3. Sauté Carrots: Add the shredded carrots and a splash of water to the pan. Sauté for 3-4 minutes until tender.
  4. Combine: Return the crispy bacon to the pan. Add the green onion tops, parsley, cilantro, and salt. Stir to combine.
  5. Wilt Kale: Add kale in 2-3 batches, stirring to wilt and combine between each addition.
  6. Serve: Taste and adjust for salt. Serve warm, optionally with sliced avocado.

Portioning Guide

Alexander: 2 generous cups of hash.

Hanna: 1.25 cups of hash.

Notes

This is a great alternative to the standard turkey hash. Yukon Golds provide a creamy texture that pairs perfectly with the salty bacon and fresh herbs.
